title: Conjecture 1 (Harutyunyan–Mohar)
statement_text: Let $D$ be a digraph of digirth at least $3$ and maximum degree $\Delta$. Then $\vec{\chi}(D) \leq \left\lceil \frac{\tilde{\Delta}(D)}{2} \right\rceil + 1 \leq \left\lceil \frac{\Delta}{4} \right\rceil + 1$.
context_text: The conjecture claims that the Brooks-type bound $\vec{\chi}(D) \leq \lceil \frac{\Delta}{2} \rceil$ for digraphs of digirth at least 3 can be improved by a factor of 2 when directed 2-cycles are forbidden. Here $\tilde{\Delta}(D) = \max\{\sqrt{d^+(v)d^-(v)} \mid v \in V(D)\}$ is the maximum geometric mean of in- and out-degree. Harutyunyan and Mohar proved a weaker $(1-\varepsilon)\tilde{\Delta}(D)$ bound; Golowich subsequently showed $\vec{\chi}(D) \leq \frac{2}{5}\Delta(D) + O(1)$.
paper_contribution: Proves that for every integer $g \geq 2$, every digraph $D$ with digirth at least $2g-1$ and maximum degree $\Delta$ satisfies $\vec{\chi}(D) \leq \left(\frac{1}{3} + \frac{1}{3g}\right)\Delta + (g+1)$, improving Golowich's multiplicative constant for digraphs of digirth at least $11$ (i.e.\ $g \geq 6$).
notes: PDF source — math notation partially garbled in extraction; statement reconstructed from context in abstract and body. The ceiling notation appeared as 'l m' artifacts in the raw PDF text.
paper_title: A Note on Coloring Digraphs of Large Girth
